steps.title: Identify what the passage emphasizes
steps.content: The passage opens by stating that women like Grace Murray Hopper made important early contributions to cryptology, then details Hopper's specific achievements, and closes by noting how Hopper and others helped more women — such as Dr. Loyce Pailen — enter the field. The central point is that women have contributed to cryptology, historically and today.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
Test each choice against the passage's central focus on women's contributions to cryptology:
  • Choice A — 'Cryptology should be taught more often in schools to encourage more women to enter the field.': the passage does not argue for a policy change or make any recommendation about school curriculum. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Women such as Grace Murray Hopper and Dr. Loyce Pailen have contributed to the field of cryptology.': directly matches the passage's opening claim and the evidence provided throughout. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Cryptology is a field that focuses primarily on securely managing data.': this is a definition mentioned briefly in passing, not the main idea of the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Grace Murray Hopper and Dr. Loyce Pailen worked together on an important project in the field of cryptology.': the passage never states these two women worked together; Pailen is introduced only as an example of a woman who entered the field later. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
